questions.question: Where does all energy in a food chain originally come from?
questions.questionThai: พลังงานทั้งหมดในห่วงโซ่อาหารมาจากไหนในตอนแรก?
questions.explanation: All energy in food chains originally comes from the sun. Plants capture this energy through photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is a producer in a food chain?
questions.questionThai: ผู้ผลิตในห่วงโซ่อาหารคืออะไร?
options: A living thing that makes its own food
questions.explanation: A producer is a living thing that makes its own food, usually a plant through photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is a consumer?
questions.questionThai: ผู้บริโภคคืออะไร?
options: A living thing that eats other living things
questions.explanation: A consumer is a living thing that cannot make its own food and must eat other living things.

questions.question: What is a herbivore?
questions.questionThai: สัตว์กินพืชคืออะไร?
options: An animal that eats only plants
An animal that eats only meat
questions.explanation: A herbivore is an animal that only eats plants.

questions.question: What is a carnivore?
questions.questionThai: สัตว์กินเนื้อคืออะไร?
options: An animal that eats only plants
An animal that eats only other animals
questions.explanation: A carnivore is an animal that only eats other animals.

questions.question: What do decomposers do?
questions.questionThai: ผู้ย่อยสลายทำอะไร?
options: Break down dead plants and animals
questions.explanation: Decomposers break down dead plants and animals and return nutrients to the soil.

questions.question: In this food chain: Grass - Rabbit - Fox, what is the rabbit?
questions.questionThai: ในห่วงโซ่อาหารนี้: หญ้า - กระต่าย - สุนัขจิ้งจอก กระต่ายคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: The rabbit is a primary consumer because it eats the producer (grass).

questions.question: Which of these is a decomposer?
questions.questionThai: ข้อใดต่อไปนี้เป็นผู้ย่อยสลาย?
questions.explanation: Mushrooms are decomposers that break down dead organic matter.

questions.question: Why are producers always at the beginning of a food chain?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมผู้ผลิตจึงอยู่ที่จุดเริ่มต้นของห่วงโซ่อาหารเสมอ?
options: Because they capture energy from the sun first
questions.explanation: Producers are first because they capture the sun's energy and make it available to other living things.

questions.question: What happens to energy as it moves through a food chain?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นกับพลังงานเมื่อมันเคลื่อนที่ผ่านห่วงโซ่อาหาร?
options: Some energy is lost at each level
questions.explanation: Energy decreases at each level. Only about 10% passes to the next level; the rest is used for living.

questions.question: What is a food web?
questions.questionThai: สายใยอาหารคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: A food web is many connected food chains in an ecosystem, showing how organisms are connected in multiple ways.

questions.question: What is an omnivore?
questions.questionThai: สัตว์กินทั้งพืชและสัตว์คืออะไร?
options: An animal that eats only plants
An animal that eats only meat
An animal that eats both plants and animals
questions.explanation: An omnivore eats both plants and animals. Humans and bears are examples of omnivores.

questions.question: In the reading passage, what ate the rice plants?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน อะไรกินต้นข้าว?
questions.explanation: In the reading passage, planthoppers were eating the rice plants.

questions.question: Why are there usually more plants than herbivores in an ecosystem?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมมักจะมีพืชมากกว่าสัตว์กินพืชในระบบนิเวศ?
options: It takes many plants to provide enough energy for herbivores
questions.explanation: Since energy is lost at each level, many plants are needed to support fewer herbivores.

questions.question: What would happen if all decomposers disappeared?
questions.questionThai: จะเกิดอะไรขึ้นถ้าผู้ย่อยสลายทั้งหมดหายไป?
options: Dead things would pile up and nutrients wouldn't return to soil
questions.explanation: Without decomposers, dead organisms would pile up and nutrients wouldn't be recycled back to the soil for plants.

questions.question: In the reading passage, how did Ploy's family fix the food chain problem?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน ครอบครัวของปลอยแก้ปัญหาห่วงโซ่อาหารอย่างไร?
options: Brought spiders back by stopping chemicals and planting flowers
questions.explanation: They stopped using chemicals and planted flowers to attract helpful insects, allowing spiders to return and control planthoppers.

questions.question: Why do food chains usually have only 4-5 links?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมห่วงโซ่อาหารมักจะมีเพียง 4-5 ข้อต่อ?
options: Energy decreases at each level so there isn't enough for more levels
questions.explanation: Since only about 10% of energy passes to each level, there isn't enough energy left to support more than 4-5 levels.

questions.question: What happens when one part of a food chain is removed?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นเมื่อส่วนหนึ่งของห่วงโซ่อาหารถูกนำออกไป?
options: The whole food chain and ecosystem can be affected
questions.explanation: Removing one part affects all other parts. For example, removing predators causes prey populations to grow too large.

questions.question: Why are food webs more realistic than simple food chains?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมสายใยอาหารจึงเป็นจริงมากกว่าห่วงโซ่อาหารธรรมดา?
options: Animals in nature eat many different things, not just one
questions.explanation: Food webs are more realistic because most animals eat multiple food sources, not just one, creating many interconnections.

questions.question: Why is protecting all parts of a food chain important for humans?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมการปกป้องทุกส่วนของห่วงโซ่อาหารจึงสำคัญสำหรับมนุษย์?
options: Humans are part of food webs and depend on healthy ecosystems for food and resources
questions.explanation: Humans depend on healthy ecosystems for food, clean water, and other resources. Broken food chains affect us too.
